objective: Add a bounded Astralbase diagnostic for higher-support signature target generation and record whether naive rows_per_family=20 scaling is practical.
non_negotiables: The bounded diagnostic must not change existing unbounded rows_per_family=10 signature support behavior.
The report must expose candidate offsets and whether each topology family hit the candidate-pair limit.
A bounded partial result must be recorded as diagnostic only, not as a promoted shard.
Partizan ledgers must keep heuristic target promotion closed.

tasks.prompt: Add a bounded signature-support CLI/report that reuses the existing signature selector with a per-family candidate-pair limit and reports selected counts, offsets, limit-hit flags, and rejection counts.
outputs: Regression test proving the candidate-pair limit is respected
acceptance: Existing rows_per_family=10 signature tests still pass.
The bounded report completes under the 2500 candidate-pair cap.
The bounded report records candidate offsets and limit-hit flags per topology family.

tasks.prompt: Record the bounded rows_per_family=20 signature-support report, its hash, and the interpretation that naive higher-support scaling remains capped at 12/12/11 under the 2500-pair limit.
acceptance: The report records 35 selected rows, split 12/12/11 across the three topology families.
All three topology families hit the 2500 candidate-pair cap before reaching the 20-per-family target.
Docs state that this is a bounded scale diagnostic, not a promoted shard.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_42_signature_bounded_support.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 42 plan is internally consistent.
The bounded support report is valid JSON.
Astralbase bounded support tooling passes test and lint gates.
